heading: Today
text: Some patchy freezing rain near and north of the Quebec border early, then scattered showers.  A few breaks of sun south. Steadier rain late.  Highs in the 30s in the St. Lawrence, upper 30s and 40s east of the Green Mountains and north of 89, 50s to near 60 west and south.  Winds light northeast where it’s cool, south 10 to 15 mph, gusting to 25 mph in the warm air.

heading: Tonight
text: Rain this evening, tapering to showers west to east, possibly as some flurries in the St. Lawrence Valley and northern NY late.  Lows 25 to 30 in the Adirondacks and St. Lawrence, 30s, rising to the 40s in VT, and 40s in NH.  South winds 10 to 15 mph, gusting to 30 mph, shifting to the southwest late.

heading: Thursday
text: Any morning rain or snow showers, mainly east and north, tapering off.  Some breaks of sun.  Temperatures in the 20s, or falling into the 20s north, falling to the 30s south.  Winds shifting to the west 10 to 20 mph, gusting 30 to 40 mph.
